title: Red Truck Present Holder
summary: When I asked my daughter what I should make to hold presents under our tree, she said a box that looks like a red truck.
description: When I asked my daughter what I should make to hold presents under our tree, she said a box that looks like a red truck. Easy right?? Ha. I’d never seen anything like it, so I got to work!

materials.name: Plywood or MDF (optional- for details if desired), 1/4” or 1/8”
Box of 1 ¼" pocket hole screws (see tools list)
Box of Box of 1" pocket hole screws (see tools list)
Box of Kreg pocket hole plugs
Red truck design of choice (I used a design from Canva)

steps.title: Step one
steps.body:
Using your miter saw, cut the pine board so you have one 10”x 24” piece (bottom) and two 10” x 6” pieces (sides).

**Tip:** Optional 1/4” or 1/8” MDF - Details in example - handle, window, tires, hubcaps, and fenders

steps.title: Step two
steps.body: With either a jig saw or scroll saw, cut out two 30” trucks from the 1/2” birch plywood. You can trace the design onto your board to cut, or cover your material in painters tape then glue the pattern on as I did.

steps.title: Step three
steps.body: On each of the 10" x 6" sides, draw the handle to cut out. Mark 1" from the top, 2.5" from the sides, and 3.5" from the bottom and connect using your speed square to create a rectangle.

steps.title: Step four
steps.body: Drill a hole in the marked handle space big enough for your jigsaw or scroll saw blade to go through. Cut out the hole.

steps.title: Step five
steps.body: Using a round-over bit in your router, round the edges of the handles from the front and back sides.

steps.title: Step seven
steps.body: On the bottom and sides, mark where you will place your pocket holes. For the bottom, I used 3 on each long side at 6”, 12” and 18” and 2 on each sort sides at 2.5” and 6.5”. On the sides, I used 2 on each short side at 1” and 4”.

steps.title: Step eight
steps.body: Set your pocket hole depth between the 1/2” and 3/4” markers.

steps.title: Step nine
steps.body: Drill pocket holes using your Kreg Pocket Hole Jig.

steps.title: Step ten
steps.body: With the pocket holes on the bottom of the bottom piece, use your Kreg Corner clamps to attach the sides with the pocket holes on the inside using wood glue and a 1 1/4” pocket hole screw.

steps.title: Step eleven
steps.body: Once the sides and bottom are attached, line up the box along one side of your truck and clamp in place with wood glue. Attach using 1” pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Step thirteen
steps.body: Fill pocket holes with pocket hole plugs and wood glue. Use wood filler to cover any gaps.

steps.title: Step fourteen
steps.body: After the glue has dried, you are ready to paint. You can cut out other details to make your design more 3D using 1/4” or 1/8” plywood or MDF. I chose to add windows, lights, hubcaps, a door handle and fenders. This is where your creativity can shine!

steps.title: Step fifteen
steps.body: Fill with gifts and place under your tree! Follow Lesley @oakandfeatherdecor for more DIY content!
